methodology: Provider edges point from a tool that can discover/create/get a resource to a tool requiring that resource.

Exact output-parameter matches are highest confidence; resource/action-name matches and hand-authored aliases fill gaps for IDs, contacts, GitHub repositories, issues, PRs, runs, and Google Workspace resources.
Literal content inputs are marked as user/task inputs rather than tool dependencies.

nodes.label: FETCH_EMAILS
nodes.name: Fetch emails from Gmail; list messages and threads.

nodes.label: FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_MESSAGE_ID
nodes.name: Fetch a Gmail message by ID.

nodes.label: FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_THREAD_ID
nodes.name: Fetch Gmail messages in a thread.

nodes.label: CREATE_REPLY
nodes.name: Reply to an existing Gmail thread.

nodes.label: GET_CONTACTS
nodes.name: Get Google contacts for resolving people to email addresses.

nodes.label: CREATE_FILE
nodes.name: Create a Drive file or folder.

nodes.label: GET_DOCUMENT_BY_ID
nodes.name: Get a Google Doc by ID.

nodes.label: INSERT_TEXT_ACTION
nodes.name: Insert text into a Google Doc.

nodes.label: GET_SHEET_NAMES
nodes.name: List sheet names in a spreadsheet.

nodes.label: ADD_SHEET
nodes.name: Add sheet to an existing spreadsheet.
